tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I'd like to save some workspace design preferences for future reference. I have specific ideas about desk configurations, lighting, and ergonomic setups that I want to capture so I can recall them later when working on different projects. Can you help me store these preferences?

Absolutely, I'd be happy to help you store your workspace design preferences! To save them properly, I'll need you to share the specific details you'd like to capture—what are your desk configuration ideas, lighting preferences, and ergonomic setup details that you want to remember for future projects?

I have a few ergonomic setup preferences I've saved before—things like monitor height, chair adjustments, and lighting positions. Could you help me find those stored ergonomic preferences so I can reference them?
I'll search for your stored ergonomic preferences.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 10, "query": "ergonomic setup preferences monitor height chair adjustments lighting positions"}

messages.content:
It looks like there aren't any ergonomic preferences currently stored in your memory. The search came back empty, which means either they haven't been saved yet or they're stored under a different description.

Would you like to share your ergonomic setup preferences now (monitor height, chair adjustments, lighting positions, etc.)? I can save them for you so you'll be able to reference them in future projects. Just tell me the details you'd like to store!

I see that the search came back empty for the ergonomic preferences. Before I add anything new, could you show me all the design preferences I currently have stored? I want to make sure I have everything documented properly and nothing's missing.

There are currently no memories stored in your system at all—the list is completely empty. This means there aren't any design preferences (ergonomic or otherwise) currently documented.

Would you like to add your workspace design preferences now? If you'd like, you can share your desk configurations, lighting setups, and ergonomic details, and I'll save them all for future reference.

Actually, I realize we haven't stored any preferences yet—the list came back completely empty. But looking back at some of my older notes, I had a desk setup preference that I recorded incorrectly last year. It said I preferred a standing desk at 42 inches, but that was a mistake—it should be 38 inches. Could you help me remove that incorrect preference and replace it with the correct height?

I understand you want to correct a standing desk height preference from 42 inches to 38 inches. However, based on the earlier check, your memory storage is currently empty—there's no incorrect preference stored to remove.

It's possible you may be thinking of notes stored elsewhere (like a local file or another app), but nothing is currently in the memory system I have access to.

Would you like me to add the correct standing desk preference (38 inches) along with any other workspace design preferences you'd like to store? Just share the details you'd like saved, and I'll add them for you.

You're right, it seems like nothing is currently stored in the system. In that case, could you just add the correct standing desk preference now? I'd like to save it as a standing desk height of 38 inches instead of that incorrect 42 inches I had recorded elsewhere. That way I'll have the accurate preference stored going forward.

Done! Your standing desk preference has been saved. The correct height of 38 inches is now stored (ID: m2) and ready for future reference.

Would you like to add any other workspace design preferences—like monitor height, chair adjustments, lighting positions, or desk configurations?
